creator: koobs@FreeBSD.org
text:
Difficult to provide an unequivocal yes/no answer to the first.

a) Relevance is subjective and contextual. Historical relevance, perhaps. relevance today, maybe not. Are there alternative sources of the same info? If so, probably, if not, maybe not.

Also, I don't believe Attachments have histories, per https://moinmo.in/HelpOnActions/AttachFile

Once deleted they are gone.

For the second:

b) The port has since been deleted, so probably, but only in terms of the statements probable original purpose, as an informal warning/liability limitation.

Other:

Fuse was committed [1] to CURRENT for FreeBSD 10 release per:

https://wiki.freebsd.org/WhatsNew/FreeBSD10#Storage_subsystems.27_improvements

The port was removed after base integration in ports r431542

The broader question: the extent to which wiki articles ought to describe the history/origins of something once they are 'final', vs just a 'current/latest state' is something we can discuss separately. Some considerations might be:

- developer/contributor credit
- record of history
- self-containedness: sources/commit log messages vs elsewhere as a canonical orauthoritative history

[1] base r241519
